threatgraph_indicators.description: Service Control Manager executed a file written by SMB. Adversaries can use lateral movement to execute malicious files from remote systems. Review the file and process tree.
A user executed an unusual file. Adversaries can lure users into executing malicious code using spearphishing and other trickery. Review the executable and process tree.

A process appears to be using a Remote Monitoring and Management (RMM) application in a suspicious manner. This may be an attempt to gather information, gain access, or drop other malicious payloads. Investigate the process tree and the detected process.
A process has opened a handle to a Falcon process with excessive permissions requested. If this is unexpected, review the process tree and surrounding events.
A process appears to be suspiciously executing code via Powershell. Investigate the command line and process tree.
A process appears to have performed a system call by bypassing UMPPC hooks.

A process appears to be using a Remote Monitoring and Management (RMM) application in a suspicious manner. This may be an attempt to gather information, gain access, or drop other malicious payloads. Investigate the process tree and the detected process.
The command line of this process was modified from its original form prior to finalizing process creation. This might be related to command line stomping attacks. Investigate the process tree.
A process associated with ransomware modified files.

A process appears to be using a Remote Monitoring and Management (RMM) application in a suspicious manner. This may be an attempt to gather information, gain access, or drop other malicious payloads. Investigate the process tree and the detected process.
User hashes from SAM hive accessed from signed executable with untrusted root authority
